response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack/web software engineer with strong craftsmanship practices and some low-latency backend experience, but the role requires deep hardware-level expertise — SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appears in the resume. While the years-of-experience requirement is met, the core domain mismatch is fundamental for a Principal Performance Architect position.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (DLang, async I/O, fibers at millions of requests/sec)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions, and teaching/mentoring
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon, or hardware architecture experience
No AI accelerator, GPU workload analysis, or deep-learning training/inference optimization background
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, not principal-level performance architecture

key_factors.factor: No AI/DL workload or GPU architecture experience
